methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
Deep Atomic is a pre-seed nuclear energy company founded by William G.J. Theron operating within a sector containing 3 public comparables (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

A peer-median valuation approach is not applicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparable data. The nuclear energy sector contains 8 private firms total, but applying filters for stage alignment and valuation tier yields fewer than three datapoints necessary for meaningful statistical inference. An all-sector median would not reflect the specific risk and development profile of early-stage nuclear technology companies and is therefore not reliable for triangulation.

The limited peer visibility at the pre-seed stage in nuclear energy presents a material constraint on quantitative valuation. Public market comparables (OKLO, NNE, SMR) provide sector-level sentiment and long-term value indicators but do not anchor early-stage private valuations given differences in capital structure, development phase, and regulatory maturity. Without sufficient transaction data from same-stage private peers, conventional DCF and comparable company methods cannot be supported by the available dataset. Valuation of Deep Atomic requires either (1) accumulation of additional pre-seed nuclear financing data, or (2) alternative methods such as venture capital method calibration based on target exit multiples and probability-adjusted returns. Current data does not support a specific valuation range.
